D9 BiologyL5 EvaluateReference

Zebrafish Stripes — Turing Pattern in Vivo

zebrafish stripes emerge from local interactions between two pigment cell types (melanophore black, xanthophore yellow) — a real-world Turing reaction-diffusion system

Kondo et al. 2010 demonstrated that zebrafish stripes are a Turing pattern: melanophores activate xanthophores nearby (short range) but inhibit them long range, producing alternating stripes. Laser-ablating melanophores in a stripe disrupts the pattern — the system regenerates the original spacing after a few weeks.

Drag pattern wavelength to see different spacing. Real fish: λ ≈ 0.5 mm.
